compiled_state.title: Plugin authoring: new hooks/scripts/skills hardcode Python tooling -> silent failures on non-Python stacks
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
**Expected Behavior:** New hooks, scripts, and skills added to the DSO plugin should work across all supported stacks (Python/Flask, NextJS/TypeScript, Ruby/Rails, Ruby/Jekyll). Language-specific operations should be driven by configurable commands.* keys.

**Actual Behavior:** Contributions continue to hardcode Python-specific tooling (ruff, mypy, pytest, poetry, Python AST) with no language guard or config fallback. This causes silent failures or skipped behavior on NextJS and Ruby projects. The audit that produced Epics A–E found 30+ instances of this anti-pattern, added incrementally over time with no policy preventing recurrence.

**Suggested Fix:** Add a CLAUDE.md rule — 'This plugin is required to be polyglot. Hooks, scripts, and skills MUST NOT hardcode Python-specific tooling (ruff, mypy, pytest, poetry, Python AST). Use commands.* config keys for all language-specific operations.'

comments.body: Gate 1a: agent dispatch failed (plugin agent type limitation) — treating as ambiguous. Gate 1b: no feature-request language detected (triggered=false). Proceeding to investigation (intent-aligned via fallback path).
comments.author: Test
